meaning: n.快速而不清说的言语,乱语
examples: When he was talking to a girl he could hardly speak, and when he did speak he talked gibberish.
The robin thought he spoke this gibberish to them because they were not intelligent enough to understand feathered speech.
You were talking gibberish in your sleep.
The Autonomy web site still reads like a combo of sci-fi and we-sold-it-for-too-much gibberish.
If a computer cannot understand the context of a remark it will produce gibberish.
